---
title: "Day 2 - Block 2"
subtitle: "Data Collection"
format:
  revealjs:
    footer: "TI Workshop | D2 B2"
---

## Objective

- Design robust stimuli and instruments
- Use synthetic respondents for stress tests
- Guard against contamination and overclaiming

## Synthetic respondents: scope

- Useful: wording checks, edge-case detection
- Not sufficient: population inference, causal estimation
- Treat as pretest tool, not evidence of effects

## Contamination risks

- AI-assisted respondents in online pools
- Weak attention checks can be bypassed
- Include checks that require human grounding

## Stimulus stress-test protocol

- Generate 3 clean stimuli
- Generate 3 intentionally flawed stimuli
- Score clarity/neutrality/failure mode
- Revise and document one limitation

## Boundary rule

Use synthetic respondents for instrument stress tests only; do not infer population behavior or treatment effects.

## Hands-on (E6)

- Deliverable: 6 stimuli + limitation note + validation table
- Submission: `/docs/live-exercises?exercise=E6`

## Debrief

- Which flaw was hardest to detect?
- What cannot be replaced by synthetic responses?
- What human validation is required next?
